judgment.summary: Deere shows strong profitability and returns despite recent revenue and earnings declines, with a forward P/E suggesting expected growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: For the quarter ending 2026-01-31, Deere reported an EPS of $2.42, beating the estimate of $2.10 by 15.4%. The next earnings date is scheduled for 2026-05-21.
positives: Profit margin at 10.3% and operating margin at 17.5% indicate strong operational efficiency.
Return on equity of 19.6% demonstrates effective use of shareholder investments.
Forward P/E of 24.32x is lower than the trailing P/E of 31.54x, suggesting analysts expect earnings growth.
Current ratio of 2.25 and quick ratio of 1.99 indicate a healthy short-term liquidity position.
The mean analyst target of $665.10 suggests potential upside from the current stock price.
negatives: Revenue growth of -11.1% and earnings growth of -8.5% year-over-year indicate a recent slowdown.
Quarterly earnings growth of -9.8% and analyst estimates for current Q (-14.2%) and FY (-3.4%) point to continued expected declines.
Debt/Equity ratio of 376.02 is very high, indicating significant leverage.
Total debt of $48.47B significantly outweighs total cash of $8.83B.
PEG ratio of 1.70 suggests the stock may be overvalued relative to its expected earnings growth.

news.paragraph: Deere & Company is approaching its Q2 earnings report, with traders anticipating potential stock movement around this catalyst. While the company is being recognized by suppliers, it is also facing a new right-to-repair lawsuit concerning its construction equipment. Analysts are also weighing in on the stock, with comparisons being made to competitors like Caterpillar.
